buy_side_bullets: AI bull market has 'another year or two to run' — 1999 parallel; recently added to positions. [Tudor Jones]
US equities best time to buy quality in years; 'stupidly cheap' businesses offer 10x potential. [Ackman]
Appaloosa up 32% H1 on memory chip bets; Micron doubled as AI HBM infrastructure play with 40% cash reserve. [Tepper]
US equities long but dollar short — copper, Brazil assets, non-US markets as USD weakness hedges. [Druckenmiller]
US equities 'most expensive ever seen' — closed to new investors July 1; all 2025 alpha from gold, copper, rates. [Einhorn shift]
sell_side_bullets: Bull & Bear Indicator at 9.5 for consecutive weeks; AI bubble largest since 1880s railroad boom; Mag 7 at 48% of S&P — sell signal. [Hartnett]
AI productivity gains 'years away' — if AI underdelivers, unsustainable global debt levels become catastrophic. [Reid]
Ice Age thesis evolving: fiscal dominance + double-digit inflation risk now dominant concern, not deflation. [Edwards shift]
2026 in two stages: H1 Great Rotation (tech → non-tech), H2 broad selloff; recession probability 40-50%. [Berezin]
S&P EPS growth decelerating to just 3.3% by Dec 2026 ($262.50) — market underestimating 3 key risks. [Bannister]
Economy shifting from stagflation risk to overheating risk; 3 forces add 2.2pp to GDP. [Slok evolving]
whats_new: Reid July 7-8 piece: AI productivity 'years away' — new central thesis connecting AI delay to debt sustainability crisis

Berezin: explicit H2 broad selloff call + recession probability 40-50% — more bearish than market implies

Gundlach (buy-side) and Edwards (sell-side) AGREE rates go higher / no Fed cuts — but for different reasons (Gundlach: inflation sticky; Edwards: fiscal dominance)

aligned: AI is transformative technology but timing of productivity impact is uncertain — both sides acknowledge

Rotate out of tech/Mag 7 into non-tech in H1 2026 ahead of H2 selloff [Berezin]

Long S&P broad index with 8,250 target; rotate from AI producers to AI user beneficiaries [Yardeni]
